title: Enable GitHub Pages for Documentation
text: can enable GitHub Pages either via API manually web interface.

title: Enable GitHub Pages for Documentation
text: ’ve created script enable GitHub Pages :
code: # First, create a personal access token: # 1. Go to https://github.com/settings/tokens # 2. Click "Generate new token (classic)" # 3. Give it a name and select the `repo` scope # 4. Click "Generate token" and copy it  # Then run: export GITHUB_TOKEN='your_token_here' ./enable_github_pages.sh

title: Enable GitHub Pages for Documentation
text: prefer run API command directly:
code: curl -X POST \   -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" \   -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_GITHUB_TOKEN" \   "https://api.github.com/repos/fernando-duarte/heteroskedasticity_identification/pages" \   -d '{"source":{"branch":"gh-pages","path":"/"}}'

title: Enable GitHub Pages for Documentation
text: Go repository: https://github.com/fernando-duarte/heteroskedasticity_identification Click Settings (repository navigation) Scroll Pages section (left sidebar “Code automation”) Source, select: Deploy branch Branch, select: gh-pages / (root) Click Save

title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
text: NEVER commit tokens Git! guide shows secure ways store use GitHub token.

title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
text: Create .env file project root: .env file already .gitignore (added ) Update script load .env:
code: touch .env echo "GITHUB_TOKEN=your_token_here" >> .env # The enable_github_pages.sh script already checks for GITHUB_TOKEN # Just source the .env file before running: source .env && ./enable_github_pages.sh

title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
text: Create .Renviron file R-specific usage: R:
code: echo "GITHUB_TOKEN=your_token_here" >> .Renviron # Automatically loaded when R starts Sys.getenv("GITHUB_TOKEN")

what: Option 2: Shell Configuration (Recommended for Personal Machine)
title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
text: Add shell config file (~/.zshrc ~/.bash_profile):
code: echo 'export GITHUB_TOKEN="your_token_here"' >> ~/.zshrc source ~/.zshrc

what: Option 3: macOS Keychain (Most Secure for Mac)
title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
text: Store keychain: Retrieve keychain:
code: security add-generic-password -a "$USER" -s "GITHUB_TOKEN" -w "your_token_here" export GITHUB_TOKEN=$(security find-generic-password -a "$USER" -s "GITHUB_TOKEN" -w)

title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
code: # Store in 1Password, then: export GITHUB_TOKEN=$(op item get "GitHub Token" --fields token)

title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
text: Use .env files project-specific tokens Add .env .gitignore immediately Use different tokens different projects Set token expiration dates Use minimal required scopes (just repo GitHub Pages) Rotate tokens regularly

title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
text: Commit tokens Git (even private repos) Share tokens others Use token everywhere Store tokens plain text files without .gitignore Echo tokens terminal (get saved history)

title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
code: # 1. Create .env file cat > .env << EOF GITHUB_TOKEN=your_actual_token_here EOF  # 2. Use it source .env && ./enable_github_pages.sh  # 3. For R sessions, create .Renviron cp .env .Renviron

title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
text: accidentally committed token:
code: # Check if any secrets are in git history git log -p | grep -i "token\|secret\|key\|password"  # If found, you need to: # 1. Revoke the token immediately on GitHub # 2. Use BFG Repo-Cleaner or git filter-branch to remove from history # 3. Force push (coordination needed with other contributors)

title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
text: project, need: - [x] repo - Full control private repositories Don’t add unnecessary permissions like: - [ ] admin:org - [ ] delete_repo - [ ] admin:public_key

title: Best Practices for Storing GitHub Tokens
text: Create helper script setup_env.sh: Remember: Security convenient, ’s necessary! 🔒
code: #!/bin/bash if [ ! -f .env ]; then     echo "Creating .env file..."     echo "Please enter your GitHub token:"     read -s token     echo "GITHUB_TOKEN=$token" > .env     echo "✅ .env file created" else     echo "✅ .env file already exists" fi

text: Open R session (e.g., RStudio via Rscript) run:
code: # Install from CRAN install.packages("rworkflows")  # (Optional) Install the latest development version: # remotes::install_github("neurogenomics/rworkflows")

title: NA
text: templateR GitHub repo provides CRAN-compatible scaffold. Merge necessary files:
code: # 1. Add templateR as a temporary remote git remote add templateR https://github.com/neurogenomics/templateR.git git fetch templateR  # 2. Check out scaffold files into your branch git checkout templateR/main -- \   DESCRIPTION \   NAMESPACE \   inst/ \   man/ \   R/000-package.R \   .github/workflows/templateR.yaml  # 3. Remove the temporary remote git remote remove templateR

title: NA
text: Open DESCRIPTION editor merge update fields: Also ensure LICENSE file (e.g., MIT) CRAN-related metadata (e.g., cran-comments.md desired).
code: Package:        YOURPACKAGENAME Title:          A Package for X in Econometrics Version:        0.1.0 Authors@R:      person("First", "Last",                       email = "you@example.com",                       role = c("aut", "cre")) Description:    Brief description of what your package does. License:        MIT + file LICENSE Encoding:       UTF-8 LazyData:       true Roxygen:        list(markdown = TRUE)

title: NA
text: Back R, run: creates updates .github/workflows/rworkflows.yaml : Run R CMD check across platforms Build vignettes pkgdown site (Optionally) Build push Docker container Perform Bioconductor checks, Python support, etc.
code: rworkflows::use_workflow(template = "templateR")

title: NA

GitHub Actions, confirm see rworkflows job run successfully pkgdown site (configured) builds/deploys. 🎉 repository now : CRAN-compatible package scaffold templateR modern rworkflows GitHub Action pipeline (checks, docs, Docker, Bioconductor) Roxygen2, testthat, vignettes, pkgdown configured ’re ready continue developing econometrics data-analysis package automated, reproducible CI/CD place!
